---
pagetitle: "David Maguire — Quantitative & AI Research Notebook"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.hero-row}
::: {.hero-text}
# David Maguire

::: {.lead}
A public research notebook documenting the mathematics, models, and experiments
behind my work in quantitative finance, machine learning, and AI. The aim is
simple: to demonstrate understanding rather than claim it — a record of research
capability I am building as I develop a PhD proposal.
:::
:::

::: {.hero-motif}
<svg width="230" height="120" viewBox="0 0 230 120" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" aria-hidden="true">
<line x1="6" y1="100" x2="122" y2="100" stroke="#5f83a8" stroke-width="1.2" stroke-opacity=".8"/>
<path d="M10 98 C 38 98 46 26 66 26 C 86 26 94 98 122 98" stroke="#2c5f8a" stroke-width="1.4" stroke-opacity=".85"/>
<line x1="150" y1="38" x2="194" y2="50" stroke="#5f83a8" stroke-width="1" stroke-opacity=".5"/>
<line x1="150" y1="38" x2="194" y2="72" stroke="#5f83a8" stroke-width="1" stroke-opacity=".5"/>
<line x1="150" y1="60" x2="194" y2="50" stroke="#5f83a8" stroke-width="1" stroke-opacity=".5"/>
<line x1="150" y1="60" x2="194" y2="72" stroke="#5f83a8" stroke-width="1" stroke-opacity=".5"/>
<line x1="150" y1="82" x2="194" y2="50" stroke="#5f83a8" stroke-width="1" stroke-opacity=".5"/>
<line x1="150" y1="82" x2="194" y2="72" stroke="#5f83a8" stroke-width="1" stroke-opacity=".5"/>
<line x1="194" y1="50" x2="222" y2="61" stroke="#5f83a8" stroke-width="1" stroke-opacity=".5"/>
<line x1="194" y1="72" x2="222" y2="61" stroke="#5f83a8" stroke-width="1" stroke-opacity=".5"/>
<circle cx="150" cy="38" r="4.2" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#2c5f8a" stroke-width="1.3"/>
<circle cx="150" cy="60" r="4.2" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#2c5f8a" stroke-width="1.3"/>
<circle cx="150" cy="82" r="4.2" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#2c5f8a" stroke-width="1.3"/>
<circle cx="194" cy="50" r="4.2" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#2c5f8a" stroke-width="1.3"/>
<circle cx="194" cy="72" r="4.2" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#2c5f8a" stroke-width="1.3"/>
<circle cx="222" cy="61" r="4.2" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#2c5f8a" stroke-width="1.3"/>
</svg>
:::
:::
:::

## The libraries

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .section-card}
#### <i class="bi bi-person card-ic" aria-hidden="true"></i> [About](about.qmd)
Background in banking analytics, my quant-finance interests, and the AI research direction I'm heading toward.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .section-card}
#### <span class="card-ic sigma" aria-hidden="true">&#8721;</span> [Equation Library](equations/index.qmd)
The mathematical foundations of quant finance and machine learning — each concept taken from equation to plain English to Python.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .section-card}
#### <i class="bi bi-diagram-3 card-ic" aria-hidden="true"></i> [Machine Learning Library](ml/index.qmd)
Models explained from first principles: their assumptions, how they learn, where they fail, and how they apply to markets.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .section-card}
#### <i class="bi bi-bar-chart-line card-ic" aria-hidden="true"></i> [Quant Research Lab](quant-lab/index.qmd)
Reproducible strategy experiments with explicit hypotheses, methods, and honestly reported results — including the ones that don't work.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .section-card}
#### <i class="bi bi-robot card-ic" aria-hidden="true"></i> [AI Trading Systems](ai-systems/index.qmd)
How LLMs and AI agents could support market decision-making — and the risk controls that make such a system credible.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .section-card}
#### <i class="bi bi-mortarboard card-ic" aria-hidden="true"></i> [PhD Research](phd/index.qmd)
Developing a research proposal in the open: research questions, literature notes, methodology, and supervisor alignment.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .section-card}
#### <i class="bi bi-code-slash card-ic" aria-hidden="true"></i> [Code Repository](code.qmd)
GitHub repositories and reproducible notebooks behind every experiment on this site.
:::

:::

## How I decide what to publish

Nothing goes on this site until I can answer six questions about it without notes:
what it means in plain English, why it matters in quant finance, what the equation
or model is actually doing, what it assumes, where it can fail, and how I would
implement it in Python. If I can't answer those, the material stays private until
I can. The goal is demonstrated understanding, not coverage.
